2026-06-11.patch_summary: This patch adds four new macros, SCHED_WARN_ON(), SCHED_WARN_ON_ONCE(), SCHED_WARN(), and SCHED_WARN_ONCE(), which are similar to the existing WARN_*() macros but bracket their output in a printk_deferred section. This prevents potential deadlocks when the current CPU already holds an rq->lock or a ->pi_lock. The new macros behave exactly like their WARN_*() counterparts, except that they defer console output to irq_work instead of emitting it synchronously. The patch also includes a lockdep_assert_preemption_disabled() guard to prevent misuse from preemptible context.
